raw_response: I agree with the selection of option C, which recommends reverse total shoulder arthroplasty as the primary treatment for this case. One additional point to support this choice is that while tendon transfers can sometimes work, they may not provide sufficient functional recovery compared to modern joint replacements like reverse total shoulder arthroplasties, especially given the emphasis on maintaining high levels of mobility and strength typical in younger individuals.
